title: rudbeckia serotina f. annulata
intro: Rudbeckia serotina f. annulata, also known as the yellow coneflower, is a perennial plant species within the sunflower family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers with dark centers and a fern-like foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Rudbeckia serotina f. annulata include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and support immune function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for various health conditions. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which are commonly used in herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
